back_translation: Pursue love earnestly, and desire spiritual gifts seriously — especially that you may prophesy.
Because the one who speaks in various languages does not speak with humans, but speaks with God; because no one understands him; he speaks mysteries by the Spirit.
But the one who prophesies speaks with humans for the building-up, the encouragement, and the consolation.
The one who speaks in various languages builds up himself; but the one who prophesies builds up the church.

I want all of you to be able to speak in various languages, but I want you to prophesy more than that. The one who prophesies is greater than the one who speaks in various languages — unless he interprets, so that the church may receive building-up.

Now, all you brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in various languages, what benefit will I be to you, if I do not speak with you in the form of revelation, or knowledge, or prophecy, or teaching?

Even lifeless objects that produce sound — whether the flute or the lyre — if they do not give sounds that are clearly distinct, how will anyone know what is being played on the flute or what is being played on the lyre?
Likewise, if the trumpet sounds unclearly, who will prepare himself to go out to battle?

In the same way as you all — if you all do not speak words that can be understood through the mouth, how can people know what you are saying? You all will become those who speak floating into the air.
In this world there are many different languages, and not one of these languages is without meaning.
Therefore, if I do not understand the meaning of that language, I am also a foreigner to the speaker, and the speaker is also a foreigner to me.
In the same way as you all — since you all desire greatly the spiritual gifts, then strive to grow in the gifts that build up the church.
Therefore, the one who speaks in various languages, let him pray in order that he may be able to interpret.
Because if I pray in various languages, my spirit prays — but my mind is unfruitful.
Then what shall I do? I will pray with my spirit, and I will also pray with my mind. I will sing with my spirit, and I will also sing with my mind.

Otherwise, if you bless with your spirit, the one who has no knowledge in this matter who is sitting present, how will he say "Amen" along with your thanksgiving? Because he does not know what you are saying.
Because although you may give thanks well, the other person does not receive building-up.
I myself thank God that I speak in various languages more than all of you.
But in the church, I want to speak five words with my own understanding, in order to teach others, rather than to speak ten thousand words in various languages.
All you brothers and sisters, do not think like a child; rather, be like an infant in matters of evil, and think like a mature person.

In the Law there is a written word that says: "I will speak with this people through people speaking strange languages and through the lips of foreigners; even so, they will not listen to me. The (royal) Lord says this."
Therefore, the various languages are a sign — not for those who believe, but for those who do not believe. But prophecy — not for those who do not believe, but for those who believe.
Therefore, if the entire church gathers together, and everyone speaks in various languages, and people who have no knowledge or who do not believe enter — will they not say that you are insane?
But if everyone prophesies, and someone who does not believe or who has no knowledge enters, he will be shown his wrongdoing by the words of everyone; he will be examined by everyone.
The secrets within his heart will become evident; therefore he will fall down to (royally) worship God, proclaiming, "God truly (royally) dwells among you all."

All you brothers and sisters, what then shall we do? When you all gather together, each one has a song of praise, has teaching, has revelation, has various languages, has interpretation of languages. Let everything be done for the purpose of building-up.
If anyone speaks in various languages, let two people speak, or three at the most, taking turns one at a time; and let one person interpret as well.
But if there is no interpreter, let him keep silent in the church; and let him speak only with himself and with God.
As for the prophets, let two or three speak, and let the others examine what is said.
And if revelation comes to another person who is sitting, let the first speaker stop.
Because all of you are able to prophesy one at a time, in order that all may learn, and all may receive encouragement.
And the spirits of the prophets are under the control of the prophets themselves.
Because God is not the God of disorder, but is (royally) the God of peace — as is the case in the churches of all the holy ones.
Let the women keep silent in the churches, because it is not permitted for them to speak; rather, let them be in submission, just as the Law has said.
If they desire to learn anything, let them ask their own husbands at home; because it is shameful for a woman to speak in the church.
Or did the word of God come out from you all? Or has that word reached only you all?
If anyone considers himself to be a prophet or a spiritual person, let him acknowledge that what I write to you all is the (royal) command of the Lord.
But if anyone refuses to acknowledge, that one will not receive acknowledgment.
Therefore, my brothers and sisters, desire to prophesy; and do not forbid the speaking in various languages.
But let everything be done in an appropriate manner and in order.
